materialitySignals: new customer promise and complete producer workflow
new strict terrain document and deterministic procedural pixel output
project graph, IndexedDB, folder, snapshot, and archive compatibility changes
new archive format with old-reader fail-closed behavior
new generic and Godot 4.7.1 downstream artifacts
shared web, packaged Electron, offline, and cross-host parity claims
retained character, content-pack, security, and release baselines

dimensions.definitionOf10: The update proves the roadmap terrain outcome for an indie developer with observable create, customize, persist, transfer, and engine-ready delivery results while preserving the character producer.

One optional built-in static terrain document is shown to be the smallest coherent second producer, with packs, scenes, transitions, animation, structures, arbitrary maps, and token redesign explicitly rejected or deferred.
Host-neutral terrain types, renderer, persistence, archive v3, exports, UI ownership, and unchanged bridge boundaries are exact and fit the existing project graph without hidden authority.

Terrain validation, revisions, snapshots, migration, atomic commits, archive inventories, old-reader rejection, external modification, invalid drafts, and rollback all name exact no-partial-mutation behavior.

Correctness, determinism, compatibility, durability, conflict safety, accessibility, performance, downstream validity, offline operation, security, recovery, and rollback have categorical measurable scenarios.

All promises, flows, 28 behaviors, 64 material-mask fixtures, host paths, compatibility obligations, Godot consumer checks, performance thresholds, rollback, and inherited external gates have exact evidence requirements.

A fresh implementer can execute seven dependency-gated work packages without choosing product behavior, deterministic bytes, storage topology, archive semantics, engine metadata, error ownership, or UX recovery behavior.

Empty, create, material, palette, paint, erase, fill, removal, export, invalid, conflict, recovery, keyboard grid, focus, reflow, targets, contrast, and reduced-motion behavior are explicit and testable.
Generic consumers and Godot 4.7.1 users receive deterministic documented artifacts that require no manual slicing or peering reconstruction, with a pinned downstream fixture.
Strict bounded terrain JSON and archive paths retain the existing local-only trust model, Electron grants, data-only boundary, pre-mutation validation, and zero telemetry/network authority.

Interrupted saves, stale revisions, quota/I/O failures, external edits, malformed archives, feature withdrawal, recovery snapshots, and reader-only rollback preserve last-known-good character and terrain state.
Document, map, tile, atlas, JSON, interaction, render, export, and main-thread boundaries are numeric and map to ordinary and one-over fixtures on both hosts.
DB v3-to-v4, graph null normalization, archive v1/v2 preservation, archive v3 mandatory terrain, old-reader timing, cross-host transfer, and rollback reader preservation are exact and testable.
evidence: quality-scenarios.md QS-304 through QS-307 and QS-315

dimensions.definitionOf10: Web and Electron readers/writers release together, offline and packaged evidence is mandatory, feature withdrawal retains readers and data, and inherited actual Pages/clean-machine gates remain truthful external obligations.

findings.title: Terrain palette derivation did not define output bytes

evidence.claim: Round two found adjustHex without channel math, step size, or per-material role offsets.

findings.problem: Independent implementations could derive different palette bytes from the same project theme.
findings.impact: Atlas, export, and cross-host hashes could diverge.
findings.remediation: Specified immutable inputs, exact role-token offsets, uniform RGB step 12, inclusive clamping, lowercase output, and an example vector.
findings.verification: Unit vectors for all roles/materials plus repeated web/Electron atlas hash equality.

findings.title: Procedural terrain rendering was descriptive rather than executable

evidence.claim: Round two found unspecified PRNG, mark count, pixel offsets, side colors, overlap, and corner precedence.

findings.problem: A competent implementer still had to invent deterministic pixels.
findings.impact: The core visual producer, manifest hash, performance fixtures, and host parity were untestable.
findings.remediation: Defined exact xorshift32 seed/steps, twelve marks, coordinates and dimensions, absent-side bands, overwrite precedence, prohibited operations, and atlas-to-map copying.
findings.verification: All 64 material-mask pixel fixtures, repeated hashes, map-cell source equality, and actual-host cross-export comparison.

findings.title: Godot TileSet metadata and fallback strategy were unresolved

evidence.claim: Initial spec left source IDs, mode, side mapping, excluded layers, direct-load feasibility, and helper fallback open.

findings.problem: The exporter and downstream fixture required engine-format design during implementation.
findings.impact: Godot delivery could load but select wrong neighbors or require undocumented reconstruction.
findings.remediation: Pinned Godot 4.7.1; specified source/terrain IDs, atlas coordinates, MATCH_SIDES, exact N/E/S/W peering fields, exclusions, direct .tres loading, and structural fixture governance; a real probe generated one source and sixteen tiles.
findings.verification: Pinned headless fixture imports the PNG, loads source 0, and asserts tile count, region size, terrain mode/count, and representative masks.

findings.title: Archive format 3 strictness and old-reader timing needed closure

evidence.claim: Review required mandatory terrain fields/entry, character-only prohibition, malformed behavior, and the exact rejection step.

findings.problem: Readers could accept terrain-less v3 archives or parse project data before recognizing an unsupported version.
findings.impact: Compatibility could expose partial state or silently weaken archive contracts.
findings.remediation: Required terrainSchemaVersion 1 and exactly one inventoried terrain.json, forbade character-only v3, and fixed old-reader rejection at manifest step 2 before inventory or graph parsing.
findings.verification: v1/v2 byte regressions, v3 round trip, missing/duplicate terrain tamper fixtures, and baseline old-reader no-mutation test.

findings.title: Color draft and offline export ownership were implicit

evidence.claim: Round one required a precise transient invalid-text boundary and local export path.

findings.problem: Invalid color text could leak into persistence and offline export could be interpreted as service-worker/network work.
findings.impact: Users could reopen invalid state or see inconsistent offline failures.
findings.remediation: Made invalid text component-local and non-dirty/non-serializable, and bound rendering/export to bundled Canvas 2D code, local materials, in-memory encoding, and browser download APIs.
findings.verification: Invalid-draft reload test and service-worker-controlled zero-request paint/save/generic/Godot export flow.

findings.title: Terrain persistence migration and desktop conflict inclusion needed exactness

evidence.claim: Review required store creation/reopen behavior and proof that terrain.json participates in the existing fingerprint.

findings.problem: An implementation could recreate/clear storage or omit external terrain edits from conflict detection.
findings.impact: Reopen durability and desktop no-overwrite guarantees could fail.
findings.remediation: Specified create-if-absent v3-to-v4 behavior, no reopen clearing, one complete graph transaction, and manifest-plus-inventory fingerprint inclusion before save.
findings.verification: DB upgrade/reopen fixtures, interruption/stale revision tests, and packaged external terrain.json conflict flow.

findings.title: Fixed 12x8 map rationale was not explicit

evidence.claim: Round one found the fixed size but not its product-boundary rationale.

findings.problem: A future reader could mistake the verification canvas for an arbitrary level-size choice.
findings.impact: Later scope discussion could reopen level-editor semantics without evidence.
findings.remediation: Recorded 12x8 as a bounded verification surface for edges, corners, holes, accessibility, persistence, and performance, not a game-level format.
findings.verification: Generated decisions and exclusions reproduce the canonical rationale and UI never exposes dimensions/import/export.

editsMade: Selected one optional built-in static terrain document as the smallest coherent next producer and excluded adjacent scene/pack/editor scope.
Created and validated the 119-record canonical update graph and complete product, UX, engineering, quality, threat, release, rollback, and evidence contracts.
Specified exact palette derivation, xorshift32 tile pixels, cardinal masks, storage transactions, archive v3, old-reader behavior, generic export, and Godot 4.7.1 metadata.
Clarified invalid color drafts, fixed-map rationale, offline export ownership, IndexedDB migration, Electron fingerprints, and character-theme isolation.
Ran a real Godot 4.7.1 ResourceSaver probe that produced one TileSet source and sixteen tiles.
Repaired all promoted findings through three unfamiliar-implementer rounds and regenerated canonical projections.

implementationSequence: WP-UPD003-SPEC: validated review, approval, generated projections, and specification lock
WP-UPD003-CONTRACTS: strict terrain types/schema, materials, masks, renderer, and baseline hash regressions
WP-UPD003-PERSISTENCE: IndexedDB v4 and Electron complete-graph save/reopen/snapshot/conflict behavior
WP-UPD003-ARCHIVE: deterministic archive v3, v1/v2 preservation, old-reader and adversarial fixtures
WP-UPD003-EXPORT: generic package, direct Godot 4.7.1 TileSet, credits, and downstream fixture
WP-UPD003-UX: terrain empty/workspace/removal/export/recovery views in web and packaged Electron
WP-UPD003-REGRESSION: actual UI, cross-host, offline, accessibility, performance, rollback, and all retained baselines
verificationGates: Traceability, generated docs, product-update, review, and lock validators pass
All 64 material-mask fixtures and palette vectors are exact and repeatable
Character-only graph, repository, archive v1/v2, renderer, generic/Godot export, packs, and credits do not drift
Archive v3 rejects tamper and old readers reject literal 3 before graph parsing or mutation
Pinned Godot 4.7.1 loads source 0 and validates sixteen tiles plus representative peering masks
All eight flows and 28 behaviors pass production web and packaged Electron evidence
Bidirectional cross-host, controlled offline, 320px/200%, performance, conflict, rollback, and inherited external release gates remain categorical

rounds.summary: The raw review incorrectly classified intentionally absent implementation as specification defects; triage retained valid color-draft, Godot, offline, fixed-map, and version-pinning clarity findings and repaired them.

rounds.reviewer: independent unfamiliar implementer with implementation-status exclusion
rounds.summary: Found genuine deterministic-byte gaps in palette and tile rendering plus archive, peering, migration, fingerprint, draft, offline, and fixture exactness; all promoted findings were repaired in canonical specs.
Verified every repaired deterministic, persistence, archive, Godot, draft, and offline contract; found no contradiction, unresolved material decision, or required product invention.

stabilization.productVisionAlignment: A deterministic built-in terrain producer proves that the local-first project can carry a structurally different second producer across web and Electron without turning the MVP into a scene editor or weakening character compatibility.
